fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue with high future estimates, but currently unprofitable and trading at a high forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates (-0.07 vs -0.05); next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current Q: 109.4% — very strong projected earnings growth.
Analyst growth est, current FY: 441.7% — massive expected earnings growth.
Forward P/E 38.91x, PEG ratio 0.78x — growth may justify the premium valuation.
Current ratio 3.83x, Quick ratio 2.85x — strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6%, Profit margin -8.5% — currently loss-making.
Return on equity -6.1%, Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability metrics.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — burning significant cash.
Debt/Equity: 25.36 — high leverage relative to equity.
Mean target $151.30 vs spot $185.67 — analysts see downside from current levels.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant volatility, with recent reports highlighting its impressive 439% year-to-date gain, strong demand from data centers and AI computing, and a subsequent 9% tumble as optics stocks cooled. Despite the recent pullback, analysts remain largely positive with a consensus 'buy' rating, though price targets vary widely. The company's strong revenue growth and analyst estimates for current quarter and fiscal year earnings suggest continued investor interest, but the negative margins and free cash flow raise concerns.
